title: geum brachypetalum
intro: Geum brachypetalum, also known as the small-flowered avens, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in flavonoids, which provide several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Geum brachypetalum can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
